subtitle: Exit Through the Gift Shop
quote: The map was never about finding a destination. It was about what you became along the way.
format_note: intentional B&W halftone — different aesthetic from the rest of the deck, marks Elicia as observer/recorder rather than actor

ciphers.text: stack of VHS tapes labeled 'Anya' on the upper shelf
ciphers.encoding_hint: Anya is not yet a named character in the project — these tapes are forward-seeding. Multiple tapes suggests Anya is documented/recorded by Elicia. Possible vol6 or vol7 reveal.
ciphers.priority: HIGH — first canonical appearance of this character

ciphers.text: circular badge in lower-right reading 'MODERN MYTHOLOGY · NARRATIVE STRUCTURE COMPASS (HIGH PRIESTESS Node)'
ciphers.encoding_hint: fourth-wall — Elicia's card explicitly identifies itself as a node in the project's narrative structure compass. Implies the deck itself is a meta-system the player can navigate. Touching this badge in-game could unlock a dev-notes/structure overlay or the alt-ending branch.

ciphers.text: journal labeled 'PERSONAL TAROT SYMBOLOGY NOTES' on desk
ciphers.encoding_hint: Elicia's personal interpretation of the major arcana — could become a collectible journal in-game with her annotations on every card (each annotation = lore drop for that arcana)

her source material — book titles reveal she's studying the same systems the project itself uses. 'OCCULT SYMBOLOGY of the ASCENDANT' = key for the meta-compass; the ASCENDANT is another arcana keyword to watch for

ciphers.text: computer monitor showing video-editing software w/ waveform
ciphers.encoding_hint: she's editing audio/video — the waveform could be matched against the project's actual voice clips to identify which scene she's reviewing. Possible audio-cipher: scrubbing the waveform plays a hidden line.

ciphers.text: pomegranate sigil embossed on the closed leather journal beside her tarot notes
ciphers.encoding_hint: pomegranate is the High Priestess's traditional veil-pattern — matches the existing high_priestess_arcana procedural rendering

ciphers.text: swampy cypress visible through the window — Graustark setting
ciphers.encoding_hint: establishes location continuity with vol5_ch3_empress (also Graustark) and vol5_ch5_hierophant (Acadian)

hotspots.notes: clicking the tapes opens an inventory/list of tape labels — primary reveal vector for Anya as a character
meta unlock — could reveal the narrative structure overlay (where each arcana sits in the story graph) or a dev-commentary track

easter_eggs: the priestess card is the ONLY one in halftone B&W — marks her as the observer/archivist rather than an actor. She RECORDS, doesn't act.
Anya tapes are forward-seeded — character name introduced visually before any prose mentions her. When she eventually appears in dialogue, players who clicked the tapes get a 'recognition' beat.
the NARRATIVE STRUCTURE COMPASS badge is the most explicit fourth-wall element in the entire deck. Game-design-wise it argues for an unlockable 'structure view' showing the arcana network
books on the shelf reference the actual study system the player is engaged in — Elicia is the in-fiction scholar of the same tarot the game uses for chapters
Anya tape labels visible: at least two cassettes — could be authored as Anya_1.tape, Anya_2.tape inventory items
the crystal shards on the desk could be glass from a broken mirror — paired with the Emperor card's mirror-reflection cipher = the dual_pov_node lineage
